description: Registry of all rule sets available to the EHC Checker engine. Each entry describes one rule set (a commodity + origin + destination combination) and the certificate types it handles. The engine reads this file at request time to select the appropriate rule set for an incoming certificate. To add a new rule set, create a new folder under rules/ and add an entry here. No engine code changes required.

ruleSets.name: UK Dairy Exports to EU
ruleSets.author: Dr. RR Cunningham MRCVS (SP 136830)

certificateTypes.name: EHC 8468 — Dairy products for human consumption (DAIRY-PRODUCTS-PT)
certificateTypes.description: UK dairy products for human consumption requiring pasteurisation treatment. Covers standard EU export routes via Calais (SIVEP), Esbjerg, Rotterdam, Rosslare, Dublin, Belfast. Header: 'Dairy products intended for human consumption that are required to undergo a pasteurisation treatment'. Part II header: 'Certificate model DAIRY-PRODUCTS-PT'. I.20 'Products for human consumption' must be ticked.

headerKeywords: Dairy products intended for human consumption

certificateTypes.name: EHC 8322 — Milk products not for human consumption (Category 3 ABP)
certificateTypes.description: UK milk, milk-based and milk-derived products NOT for human consumption. Category 3 Animal By-Product (ABP). Header: 'Milk, milk-based products and milk-derived products not for human consumption'. I.25 has four tickboxes (Animal feedingstuff, Further process, Production of petfood, Technical use) — exactly one must be ticked. I.20 is blank on 8322. Key 8322 differences: I.25 tickboxes mandatory, I.12 Cat 3 approval number should be present, I.28 production dates not required, Part II preamble requires product-type deletion.

ruleSets.notes: Both certificate types share the same rule_set.md document. Claude reads the full markdown rule set on every check and applies Parts A + B + D + E for 8468 certificates and Parts A + B + C + E for 8322 certificates. This shared structure reflects how Dr. Cunningham organised the rule set.
